Gradient boosted propensity model (XGBoost)

Source: R/propensity.R
propensity_model_gradient_boosted.Rd

Propensity model using xgboost::xgb.train / xgboost::xgb.DMatrix or xgboost::xgb.Booster for binary classification. Optional early stopping. Requires the xgboost package. Mirrors Python GradientBoostedPropensityModel.

Usage

propensity_model_gradient_boosted(
  clip_bounds = c(0.001, 1 - 0.001),
  calibrate = TRUE,
  early_stop = FALSE,
  stop_val_size = 0.2,
  max_depth = 8L,
  learning_rate = 0.1,
  n_estimators = 100L,
  colsample_bytree = 0.8,
  random_state = 42L,
  ...
)

Arguments

clip_bounds

length-2 numeric: bounds for clipping.

calibrate

logical: whether to calibrate (default TRUE).

early_stop

logical: whether to use a validation set for early stopping (default FALSE).

stop_val_size

numeric: fraction of data for validation when early_stop=TRUE (default 0.2).

max_depth, learning_rate, n_estimators, colsample_bytree, random_state

passed to xgboost; defaults mirror Python.

...

other arguments passed to xgboost::xgb.train or xgboost::xgb.Booster.

Value

Propensity model object with fit, predict, fit_predict.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# propensity_model_gradient_boosted(...)
} # }
